name: Philoctetes
description: Philoctetes was a renowned Greek hero, celebrated for his unparalleled skill in archery. He was the son of Poeas, king of Meliboea in Thessaly. His destiny became intertwined with the Trojan War when, en route to Troy with the Greek fleet, he was bitten by a snake while resting on the island of Chryse. The wound festered and emitted a terrible odor, causing the Greeks to abandon him on the desolate island of Lemnos. For ten long years, Philoctetes endured immense suffering, sustained only by his bow, which had been gifted to him by Heracles for lighting Heracles' funeral pyre. The Greeks, however, could not conquer Troy without Philoctetes and Heracles' bow, as an oracle had foretold. Odysseus and Neoptolemus eventually sailed to Lemnos to retrieve him. Despite his initial resentment and distrust, Philoctetes was persuaded to join them, his wound miraculously healed by Asclepius' descendants. He then used Heracles' bow to kill Paris, effectively paving the way for the Greek victory at Troy. Philoctetes represents endurance, suffering, and the eventual triumph of fate and necessity.
subtitle: Master Archer, Abandoned Hero, Trojan Key
achievements: Fulfilling the Prophecy to Capture Troy
Being Healed by Machaon or Podalirius

keyMyths.title: The Snake Bite and Abandonment
keyMyths.description: Philoctetes is bitten by a snake on Chryse, and his festering wound and terrible odor lead to his abandonment on the island of Lemnos by the Greek army. This sets the stage for his decade-long isolation and suffering.

keyMyths.title: The Retrieval by Odysseus and Neoptolemus
keyMyths.description: Odysseus and Neoptolemus are tasked with retrieving Philoctetes and Heracles' bow from Lemnos, as an oracle foretold that Troy could not be taken without them. This involves deception and moral dilemmas for Neoptolemus.

keyMyths.title: The Killing of Paris
keyMyths.description: Once Philoctetes rejoins the Greek forces and is healed, he uses Heracles' bow to kill Paris, the Trojan prince who initiated the Trojan War. This act is crucial to the eventual Greek victory.

sources.author: Sophocles
sources.description: A Greek tragedy focusing on Philoctetes' suffering and the moral dilemmas faced by Odysseus and Neoptolemus.
sources.text: A Greek tragedy focusing on Philoctetes' suffering and the moral dilemmas faced by Odysseus and Neoptolemus.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: While Philoctetes' role is not central, the Iliad establishes the context of the Trojan War and the importance of certain heroes.
sources.text: While Philoctetes' role is not central, the Iliad establishes the context of the Trojan War and the importance of certain heroes.

sources.author: Quintus Smyrnaeus
sources.description: A later epic poem that details events after the Iliad, including Philoctetes' role in killing Paris.
